title: India joins WTO fisheries subsidies pact, backs sustainable fishing
content:
• India became the 123rd WTO member to formally accept the World Trade Organization (WTO) Agreement on Fisheries Subsidies (AFS), which was adopted by consensus at the 12th WTO Ministerial Conference in Geneva in June 2022.
• The WTO Agreement on Fisheries Subsidies prohibits government support for illegal, unreported, and unregulated (IUU) fishing and fishing of overfished stocks, and it entered into force on 15 September 2025.
• Rajesh Agrawal, India’s commerce secretary, formally handed over India's acceptance of the WTO Agreement on Fisheries Subsidies to Ngozi Okonjo-Iweala, the director-general of the WTO, in Geneva.

title: ‘Goosebumps’: Argentina receive heroes’ welcome at home after World Cup loss; celebration goes viral | Watch
content:
• Argentina suffered a 0-1 defeat to Spain in the World Cup final.
• Captain Lionel Messi, along with players like Rodrigo De Paul and Enzo Fernandez, did not return to Argentina with the main squad.
• The returning Argentine squad landed at Ezeiza International Airport in Buenos Aires on Monday evening, having departed New York around 8 AM, Argentina time.

title: Messi unfollows Enzo Fernandez on Instagram after heated argument over World Cup loss: Social media claim goes viral
content:
• Enzo Fernandez received a red card (second yellow card) in the 93rd minute of the World Cup final after a challenge on Pau Cubarsi, leading Argentina to play with 10 players.
• Argentina lost the World Cup final to Spain with a score of 0-1, which led to the alleged heated argument in the dressing room.
• LiveMint independently verified that Lionel Messi still follows Enzo Fernandez on Instagram, debunking the viral social media claim; Fernandez is among the 368 people Messi follows.

title: Shabana Azmi, 75, felt dizzy during CJP protest, escorted to security cabin: Report
content:
• The CJP protest demanded the resignation of Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan over alleged NEET examination irregularities and broader education reforms.
• The CJP has been holding its agitation at Jantar Mantar since June 20, with the protest coinciding with the opening day of Parliament's Monsoon Session.
• Veteran actor Shabana Azmi, 75, joined thousands of protesters at Delhi's Jantar Mantar during the CJP protest.

title: Donald Trump cropped out? FIFA 's World Cup celebrating Spain's victory  sparks online buzz
content:
• Spain secured its second FIFA World Cup title in 2026, defeating Argentina 1-0 in the final after extra time, with Ferran Torres scoring the decisive goal shortly after the 106th minute.
• Spain's first FIFA World Cup triumph was in 2010, when they defeated the Netherlands 1-0 in extra time, with Andrés Iniesta scoring the winning goal in the 116th minute.
• With their 2026 victory, Spain became the seventh country to win the FIFA World Cup more than once; Brazil remains the most successful nation with five titles.

title: Pentagon rejects New York Times report on undisclosed Iran war casualties, calls allegations ‘outright lies’
content:
• The US Department of Defense rejected a New York Times report alleging it withheld information about injuries sustained by American troops during recent Iranian attacks.
• US Central Command (CENTCOM) is not required to publicly announce injuries involving service members, especially when personnel quickly return to duty, according to the report.
• The Pentagon asserts that US military casualty information is publicly available and regularly updated online through the official government channel, the Defense Casualty Analysis System.

title: Protein inflation surges as costlier fodder pushes up milk, chicken prices
content:
• Protein inflation reached 4.6% in May, up from 2.6% in January; vegetarian protein inflation rose to 3.5% in June from 2.7% in May.
• Milk was the major contributing factor to higher protein inflation in June with a 31.7% share, followed by chicken (27.6%), after companies like Amul and Mother Dairy raised prices in May.
• Madan Sabnavis, chief economist at Bank of Baroda, identified rising animal feed costs due to the West Asia conflict and heatwaves as reasons for the inflation.

title: UK trade deal: Applications invited for car import quota
content:
• India has permitted the import of 0.378 million conventional-engine passenger cars from the UK at a concessional customs duty over the first 15 years of the trade pact.
• Under the India-UK trade pact, tariffs on automotive imports will be reduced from approximately 110% to 10%.
• The Directorate General of Foreign Trade (DGFT) has invited applications for Tariff Rate Quota (TRQ) under the India-United Kingdom CETA for Calendar Year 2026 from July 21, 2026, to August 04, 2026.

title: CBIC weighs centralised GST registration under one PAN
content:
• CBIC is considering a proposal for centralised GST registration under one PAN.
• An 11-member panel, headed by chief commissioner Vinayak Chandra Gupta, will examine the legal, administrative, and technology changes needed for the framework and is to submit its report within 30 days.
• Before GST was rolled out in July 2017, service providers had the facility of centralised registration, which was then discontinued, requiring separate GST registrations in every state.

title: India joins WTO fish subsidies pact, 900 questions at trade policy review
content:
• India became a Party to the WTO Agreement on Fisheries Subsidies by depositing its Instrument of Acceptance on July 20, 2026.
• The WTO Agreement on Fisheries Subsidies, adopted at the 12th WTO Ministerial Conference in 2022, is the first multilateral WTO agreement with an environmental sustainability objective.
• India's 8th Trade Policy Review (TPR) meeting at the WTO is scheduled for July 21 and 23, during which over 900 questions were submitted by WTO members for India's responses as of July 20, 2026.
